candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Students | MD Program | Washington University in St. Louis
candidateFindings.shortQuote: Washington University only accepts applications from prospective visiting medical students through AAMC's Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO) platform.
candidateFindings.durationDecision: Limited to one four-week elective rotation

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Students | MD Program | Washington University in St. Louis
candidateFindings.shortQuote: Barnes-Jewish Hospital, St. Louis Children's Hospital, and other affiliated clinical sites

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Fees & Funding | MD Program | Washington University in St. Louis
candidateFindings.shortQuote: WashU Medicine does not charge tuition to any visiting medical student. All students applying in VSLO who are offered an elective rotation at WashU Medicine must pay a $100 administration fee.

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Application Process | MD Program | Washington University in St. Louis
candidateFindings.shortQuote: Students enrolled in international medical schools must have access to VSLO in order to apply.

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Application Process | MD Program | Washington University in St. Louis
candidateFindings.shortQuote: Applications automatically close in VSLO 6 weeks (42 days) before each rotation start date

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: International students are ineligible for WashU funding ('Students enrolled in international medical schools are not eligible for funding' — verbatim). International lane is conditional on VSLO Global / bilateral affiliation agreement, not open to all international applicants.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: Specific cost figures for international students (if any beyond the $100 admin fee) not stated on hub. Visa absent. Year-of-study language is implied by 'final year' from search summary but the direct hub fetch was less explicit.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) INSTITUTION_SPECIFIC — Barnes-Jewish + St. Louis Children's named verbatim as placement sites; (b) Final year MS, VSLO required; (c) $100 admin fee on offer + $15 AAMC VSLO fee; (d) max 1 four-week elective; (e) US LCME/AOA-only as primary lane; (f) international lane conditional on VSLO Global network or bilateral affiliation — sub-finding requires manual retry for VSLO Global affiliate list. Funding NOT available to international students.
plainEnglishSummary: WashU Medicine (Barnes-Jewish Hospital + St. Louis Children's) accepts visiting medical students through VSLO. Both hospitals are explicitly named as placement sites. No tuition charged; $100 admin fee on offer (plus $15 AAMC VSLO fee). One 4-week elective max. US lane is straightforward. International medical students can technically apply if their school is in VSLO Global or has a bilateral affiliation agreement with WashU — but they are not eligible for funding. Visa not stated.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because Missouri gained its first P101 packet covering Barnes-Jewish + St. Louis Children's with verbatim site naming and cost evidence; a nuanced international-conditional finding was captured honestly rather than over-classified as either fully open or fully closed.
